title: Sub-lemma 2c: correct invocation of MMS at n >= ck with exact constant binom(n-1,k-1)/binom(n,k)=k/n

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Obligations.**
1. From $p=\tfrac{k}{n}<\tfrac1c$ deduce $n>ck$, hence $n\ge ck$ (integers), matching the hypothesis "MMS holds for $n\ge ck$."
2. State the MMS conclusion in its **counting form**: at least $\binom{n-1}{k-1}$ of the $k$-subsets of $[n]$ have nonnegative sum, i.e. a uniform random $k$-subset $S$ satisfies $\Pr[\sum_{i\in S}z_i\ge0]\ge \binom{n-1}{k-1}/\binom{n}{k}$.
3. Compute $\binom{n-1}{k-1}/\binom{n}{k}=\tfrac{k}{n}=p$ explicitly, and verify the hypotheses ($\sum z_i=0$ from Sub-lemma 2b; $n\ge ck$) before applying. Combined with the equivalence in Sub-lemma 2b, conclude $\Pr[Y\ge p]\ge p$.
4. **Unconditional vs conditional:** for the unconditional statement ($p<10^{-46}$) cite **Pokrovskiy (2015)**'s linear bound $n\ge10^{46}k$ ($c=10^{46}$); for the conditional $p\le1/4$ statement use MMS with $c=4$ (the conjectured $n\ge4k$). Do not attempt to reprove Pokrovskiy's theorem; cite it precisely.
